confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_fa1dbd0c-2e46-42af-9930-9a73a7c6a7e0 read back as received

payload.authority_note: Operator sensed need for a stronger operator-surface message queue and execution process and requested submission.

current_gap.current_shape: operator/architect sends text -> target window receives text
missing_boundaries: message type: command, note, review request, CAPA, nudge, handoff, question
recipient admission: accepted as work, converted to task, ignored, deferred, rejected, or replied
reconciliation of message claim against task/inbox/runtime state

payload.proposed_shape: message intent -> addressed envelope -> delivery attempt -> recipient intake -> execution/admission -> reply/report -> reconciliation

requests.text: Treat operator-surface messaging as a governed crossing, not simulated keystrokes. Text delivery is only one transport for a typed message envelope.

Add a local/operator-surface message queue model with typed message records, sender identity, recipient identity, message kind, expected response posture, delivery status, intake/admission status, and evidence links.
Add recipient work-next/intake commands so agents can process queued operator-surface messages without relying only on visible terminal text.
Distinguish delivery_attempt, delivered_to_surface, admitted_by_recipient, acted, replied, and reconciled states.
Add reply/report linkage so a builder completion claim, review request, or note can be traced back to the message that caused it.
Use this queue model to prevent prior failures where informal chat/operator-surface claims were mistaken for lifecycle truth or where messages bypassed architect routing authority.

payload.summary: The current operator-surface message bridge can deliver text into agent windows, but it does not yet model messages as governed work. Narada needs a stronger queue/intake/execution process so nudges, notes, handoffs, review requests, CAPAs, and command intents are typed, tracked, admitted, and reconciled.
payload.title: Add governed operator-surface message queue and intake process
